_comment: Copy to ~/.config/weft/app.json. The canonical shape declares clusters[] (one entry per weft cluster, i.e. one etcd quorum) with nested dcs[] (one per machine room inside the cluster). Failover lives within a cluster ; switching between clusters is operator-driven (federation). The legacy flat endpoints[] shape is still accepted and auto-wrapped into a single 'default' cluster.
_auth_comment: OPTIONAL : omit the whole `auth` block to skip the sign-in window (preserves the dev / SSH-tunnel-only flow). When present, the app shows a 2-button picker (OpenPubkey / OIDC) before bringing up the tray, caches the resulting session token in the freedesktop Secret Service (GNOME Keyring / KWallet via D-Bus), and stamps Bearer on every WebView API call.
_auth_keypair_comment: OPTIONAL dev-only third auth method. Set `keypair_fallback: true` AND `gateway` to add a 3rd button (Sign in with local key (dev)) to the picker. On click, the app loads (or generates + persists) an ed25519 private key in the Secret Service (label=weft-app-keypair), signs a 60-second assertion bound to gateway/api/auth/keypair, and POSTs it ; the server returns a session id_token if the matching public key is in its --keypair-allowlist file. Register your pubkey by running `weft-loom-app-linux --print-pubkey` and pasting the output into the server's allowlist (entries[].pubkey). NEVER enable this against an Internet-facing production cluster — the server-side flag should also be unset there.

dcs.name: salle-tolbiac
dcs._note: Use 'direct' when the device is already on the WireGuard mesh ; the webui listener binds the mesh interface only.

clusters.name: tokyo
clusters._note: Second cluster, geographically distant — different etcd quorum, federated with paris through the cluster catalogue. Manual switching only ; today's flat failover pool still includes its DCs but tomorrow's per-cluster supervisor will isolate the choice to the active cluster.

_legacy_endpoints_note: Older configs that declared a flat endpoints[] without clusters[] still load — they're wrapped into a single 'default' cluster at load time. Migrate to clusters[].dcs[] for cluster-aware tray labels.
